req.title: Audit log access (boundary)
req.statement: A privacy-officer shall access audit logs scoped to their organisation; cross-organisation access shall be forbidden. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-039 mandates that a privacy-officer retrieves AuditEvent records scoped strictly to their own organisation and receives HTTP 403 when attempting cross-org access. Boundary conditions on timestamp parameters (epoch zero, max-int page size) must also be exercised. I designed four tests: (1) EP positive – officer queries own-org logs and receives 200 with matching records; (2) NEG negative – officer queries a foreign org and receives 403; (3) BVA boundary – officer queries with timestamp=0 (epoch boundary) and verifies the server accepts the request and returns scoped results; (4) BVA boundary – officer queries with _count at its maximum permitted integer boundary to ensure no overflow or mis-scoping. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create/clean up tokens and is idempotent because no persistent state is written.

tests.name: test_privacy_officer_reads_own_org_audit_events
preconditions: Authenticated privacy-officer token exists for org-A
At least one AuditEvent record exists scoped to org-A
actions: Obtain bearer token for privacy-officer in org-A via <get_token_fixture>
Assert every returned entry has agent[0].who.reference containing 'org-A'
expected: All AuditEvent entries are scoped to org-A
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/fhir/R4"
ORG_A = "org-A"

@pytest.fixture
def officer_token_org_a():
    return <get_token_fixture>(role="privacy-officer", org=ORG_A)

def test_privacy_officer_reads_own_org_audit_events(officer_token_org_a):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {officer_token_org_a}"}
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/AuditEvent",
        params={"organization": ORG_A},
        headers=headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = resp.json()
    assert bundle.get("resourceType") == "Bundle"
    entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) > 0, "Expected at least one AuditEvent for org-A"
    for entry in entries:
        resource = entry.get("resource", {})
        agent_ref = resource["agent"][0]["who"].get("reference", "")
        assert ORG_A in agent_ref, f"Cross-org record leaked: {agent_ref}"

tests.name: test_privacy_officer_denied_cross_org_audit_events
preconditions: Authenticated privacy-officer token exists for org-A
org-B is a distinct organisation with existing AuditEvent records
actions: Obtain bearer token for privacy-officer in org-A via <get_token_fixture>
Assert response body contains OperationOutcome with error code 'forbidden'
trace: R-HLT-039: cross-organisation access shall be forbidden
Error pathway: 403 when crossing org boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/fhir/R4"
ORG_A = "org-A"
ORG_B = "org-B"

@pytest.fixture
def officer_token_org_a():
    return <get_token_fixture>(role="privacy-officer", org=ORG_A)

def test_privacy_officer_denied_cross_org_audit_events(officer_token_org_a):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {officer_token_org_a}"}
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/AuditEvent",
        params={"organization": ORG_B},
        headers=headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    outcome = resp.json()
    assert outcome.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = outcome.get("issue", [])
    codes = [i.get("code", "") for i in issues]
    assert "forbidden" in codes or "security" in codes, (
        f"Expected forbidden/security issue code, got: {codes}"
    )

tests.name: test_audit_events_from_epoch_zero_timestamp_boundary
preconditions: Authenticated privacy-officer token exists for org-A
Epoch zero (1970-01-01T00:00:00Z) is the minimum valid FHIR instant
actions: Obtain bearer token for privacy-officer in org-A via <get_token_fixture>
Assert HTTP 200 (server accepts epoch-zero boundary without error)
Assert all returned entries are scoped to org-A
expected: No 400 or 422 due to epoch-zero timestamp
All entries remain scoped to org-A
trace: R-HLT-039: boundary cases on timestamps must be tested
BVA lower-bound: minimum FHIR instant value

tests.name: test_audit_events_max_count_boundary_stays_org_scoped
preconditions: Authenticated privacy-officer token exists for org-A
Maximum allowed _count value is 2147483647 (INT32_MAX) per FHIR spec
actions: Obtain bearer token for privacy-officer in org-A via <get_token_fixture>
Assert HTTP 200 or 400 (not 500); if 200 assert all entries scoped to org-A
Assert no cross-org data is returned regardless of page size
expected: HTTP 200 or 400 (server handles INT32_MAX without crashing)
If 200: all returned entries are scoped to org-A
trace: R-HLT-039: boundary cases on numeric elements must be tested
BVA upper-bound: maximum 32-bit signed integer for _count
